title: Research LilyGO built-in UI visual reference for Python TUI
description: Why this exists: the TUI visual target is 'as much as possible like the LilyGO built-in UI', but the exact device UI, screenshots, interaction model, colors, iconography, and licensing constraints need to be captured before implementation. What needs to be done: identify the relevant LilyGO firmware/UI being emulated, collect source references such as screenshots, videos, firmware docs, or code where available, summarize screen structure, status indicators, navigation model, message list behavior, compose flow, and constraints for terminal approximation. Acceptance criteria: reference device/firmware is named; source links or local source paths are recorded; key screens and visual motifs are summarized; any asset/license limits are noted; terminal approximation rules are captured for implementers.
notes:
Research completed for Python TUI visual target. Reference device/firmware: use current official Meshtastic UI (MUI) on LilyGO T-Deck / T-Deck Plus as the primary visual/workflow reference, not the older experimental/fancy UI except as historical context. Primary sources checked 2026-07-02: Meshtastic MUI docs https://meshtastic.org/docs/configuration/device-uis/meshtasticui/ ; LilyGO T-Deck Meshtastic hardware docs https://meshtastic.org/docs/hardware/devices/lilygo/tdeck/ ; meshtastic/device-ui repo https://github.com/meshtastic/device-ui at master commit 1c45ebc7433acb8ba3fe96a6f7deca9c43fa54cf ; meshtastic/firmware repo checked at HEAD 3becaf2d951ce445562ee530adee1f7811b448a6 and master 86fd2069715b38fc87dd49be3ff9a4dc7e99cc2d ; historical experimental UI context/screenshot source Jeff Geerling article https://www.jeffgeerling.com/blog/2024/realizing-meshtastics-promise-t-deck/ . Local LICHEN target context: lichen/boards/lilygo/t_deck/ and T-Deck app board fragments under lichen/apps/gateway/boards/t_deck_esp32s3_procpu.* and lichen/apps/puck/boards/t_deck_esp32s3_procpu.* . Device facts: T-Deck/T-Deck Plus is a compact handheld with 320x240 2.8 inch ST7789 IPS LCD, touchscreen, small backlit keyboard, trackball, ESP32-S3 main MCU plus ESP32-C3 keyboard MCU, SX1262 LoRa, USB-C, microphone/speaker; Plus adds GPS and 2000 mAh battery. Keyboard shortcuts from docs to reflect in terminal keymap where useful: alt+b keyboard backlight, alt+c toggles Fn modifier, Fn+m notifications, Fn+q quit/cancel, Fn+t tab/recipient/channel, Fn+i/o brightness, Fn+g GPS, Fn+space network ping. MUI screen/workflow structure to approximate: initial setup for region/long/short name; Home Dashboard as boot landing/status overview and fast navigation; Nodes List with active nodes plus filter/highlight options; Channels screen with channel encryption/status markers and channel-to-chat entry; Chats screen listing channels and DMs, unread highlight, long-press/delete analogue, threaded messages with remote-left/local-right alignment and send-status outline colors (ACK green, failed red, heard/forwarded yellow); Map screen with SD-card tile assumption, pan/zoom/current-position/home controls; Settings & Tools split into configuration and diagnostics; Tools include Mesh Detector, Signal Scanner, Trace Route, Statistics, Packet Log; Bluetooth Programming Mode/BaseUI switching are Meshtastic-specific but inform LICHEN connection-mode screens. Visual motifs: dense handheld dashboard, persistent status/navigation affordances, icon-led entry points, dark/light theme examples, short-vs-long action distinction, orange unread emphasis, green/red/yellow delivery state semantics, compact chat bubbles, device status always visible. Terminal approximation rules for implementers: do not copy bitmap assets, icon art, or exact Meshtastic/LilyGO branding; use text/Unicode/ASCII-safe symbolic labels and project-owned names. Keep a single-line status bar on every screen with connection path (BLE/IP), link state, battery, GNSS/time validity, unread count, and active route/context. Prefer a bottom command bar or compact tab strip over a touchscreen icon grid. Map touchscreen taps/long-presses to keyboard commands with visible command hints only where needed. Preserve chat alignment and delivery color semantics in terminal styling, with monochrome fallbacks using prefixes such as >, <, !, ?. Fit primary screens in 80x24; below that collapse side panels, truncate long names with stable ellipses, and put diagnostics/logs behind separate tabs. License/asset limits: meshtastic/device-ui is GPL-3.0 and uses LVGL plus SVG Repo vectors/icons; LICHEN may learn workflow and interaction patterns, but the Python TUI should not import or copy upstream generated code, screenshots, icons, SVGs, or branded assets unless separately licensed and intentionally attributed. Because LICHEN itself is not Meshtastic-compatible over RF, UI text must say LICHEN and native LCI, not Meshtastic channels/PSK, except in explicit compatibility-mode views. Acceptance met: reference firmware/device named, source links and local paths recorded, key screens/visual motifs summarized, license/asset limits noted, and terminal approximation rules captured for project-LICHEN-py4c.1.2 implementers.
Compose-flow addendum after review: MUI/T-Deck compose should be approximated as an in-context chat action rather than a separate modal-heavy form. For channels, compose starts from the selected channel/chat thread; for direct messages, compose starts from a selected node/DM or by switching the recipient/channel target from the chat context. The terminal TUI should model a compose bar/pane with target label, message text, byte/line budget, send state, and validation/error line; it should support send, cancel/back, draft preservation when switching away, target switch via a compact recipient/channel picker, and clear disabled states when disconnected, no route/recipient is selected, or payload exceeds the protocol limit. Terminal approximation: keep compose anchored at the bottom of the thread, reserve Enter/Ctrl+Enter behavior explicitly in the UX map, expose Esc/Ctrl+C as cancel/back, show pending/sent/failed/ACK/heard state inline with the message after send, and avoid touchscreen-only long-press behavior by mapping delete/retry/details to command keys or a context menu. This closes the acceptance gap noted by review Darwin: compose start, fields/states, target selection, send/cancel, and terminal compose rules are now captured for project-LICHEN-py4c.1.2.
close_reason: Completed LilyGO/T-Deck Meshtastic UI reference research for Python TUI: source links, local paths, screens, visual motifs, compose flow, terminal approximation, and license constraints recorded in Beads; two fresh reviews found no blocking findings.
